Mitigation: Independent logical recovery points, soft delete/versioning, cyber recovery copies and restore tests

Risk: Recovery region lacks quota or capacity
Mitigation: Quota reservations, capacity validation and pre-provisioned critical resources

Risk: Runbook succeeds technically but business data is inconsistent
Mitigation: Domain reconciliation rules, checkpoints, lineage and owner sign-off

Risk: CMEK or identity dependency unavailable during recovery
Mitigation: Key/identity dependency design, break-glass controls and exercises
Idempotency keys, deduplication, fencing and reconciliation

Risk: Malicious backups or contaminated recovery point
Mitigation: Immutable evidence, trusted-point selection, malware/integrity validation and clean room
Appropriate TTL, health checks, client behavior tests and manual routing option

Risk: Managed service feature or location mismatch
Mitigation: Current capability validation, service-specific tests and documented fallback
Separate recovery identities, JIT approval, dry run and audit logging

Risk: Failback causes split-brain or data loss
Mitigation: Write fencing, authoritative source decision, reverse sync and controlled validation
